issues.title: Add tags for embedded data
issues.body:
I've came over the following issue:
E.g. when feeding my vector db with multiple pdfs on different topics, e.g. K8S and some medical related topic brochure and asking LLM smth - the one started hallucinating mixing topics from K8S and medical brochure.

In this regard a request would be to add tags so that it is possible to give hints to LLM on which context the question relates to

comments.body:
The `DocumentReader `functionality has general support for adding metadata to a document during the loading phase. However, not all implementation allow adding custom metadata.

- `TextReader` -> Supported (see [example](https://github.com/ThomasVitale/llm-apps-java-spring-ai/blob/main/05-document-readers/document-readers-text-ollama/src/main/java/com/thomasvitale/ai/spring/DocumentInitializer.java#L39))
- `JsonReader` -> Supported (see [example](https://github.com/ThomasVitale/llm-apps-java-spring-ai/blob/main/05-document-readers/document-readers-json-ollama/src/main/java/com/thomasvitale/ai/spring/DocumentInitializer.java#L73))
- `PagePdfDocumentReader` and `PagePdfDocumentReader` -> No custom metadata (only default as shown [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/document-readers/pdf-reader/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/reader/pdf/PagePdfDocumentReader.java#L156) and [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/document-readers/pdf-reader/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/reader/pdf/ParagraphPdfDocumentReader.java#L164))
- `TikaDocumentReader ` -> No custom metadata (only default as shown [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/main/document-readers/tika-reader/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/reader/tika/TikaDocumentReader.java#L164))

When retrieving relevant documents, for supported vector stores (like [Chroma ](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs/chroma.html#_metadata_filtering)or [Weaviate](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/vectordbs/weaviate.html#_metadata_filtering)), you can use filter expressions to include only documents labelled with certain metadata ([example](https://github.com/ThomasVitale/spring-boot-multitenancy/blob/main/chat-service/src/main/java/com/thomasvitale/chatservice/chat/DocumentChatService.java#L44)).
